name: Garraf Buddhist Monastery and Ribes Castle Loop
name_original: Monasterio Budista desde el Castillo de Ribes - circular por el Parque del Garraf
description: This difficult 24.9-mile touring cycling route in Garraf Park features challenging terrain, Mediterranean views, and the Buddhist Monastery.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sant Pere De Ribes offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region is characterized by the rugged limestone formations of the Garraf Natural Park, featuring significant elevation changes and aromatic pine forests. Surrounding the municipality are picturesque vineyards and rolling hills typical of the Garraf and Penedès regions, providing scenic, often low-traffic roads. Additionally, Sant Pere De Ribes benefits from its proximity to the Mediterranean coast, allowing for routes that combine inland tranquility with coastal views. Best touring cycling routes around Sant Pere De Ribes The most popular touring cycling route is Garraf Buddhist Monastery and Ribes Castle Loop, a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers scenic views and cultural stops within the Garraf Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Garraf Coastal Road – Rat Penat Road loop from Escultura al collectiu homosexual, a difficult 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This route features challenging coastal roads and significant elevation gain, providing panoramic sea views. Local touring cyclists also love the Ribera Promenade – Sitges Beach and Promenade loop from Escultura al collectiu homosexual, a 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail leading through coastal promenades and beaches, often completed in about 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Sant Pere De Ribes is defined by the dramatic limestone peaks of Garraf Natural Park, extensive vineyards, and accessible coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Sant Pere De Ribes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Pere De Ribes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Sant Pere de Ribes, catering to various skill levels. These include 8 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes, offering diverse experiences from coastal rides to challenging climbs through the Garraf Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: Touring cyclists in Sant Pere de Ribes will encounter a varied landscape. The region features the rugged limestone formations of the Garraf Natural Park with significant elevation changes, picturesque vineyards and rolling hills typical of the Garraf and Penedès regions, and accessible coastal paths offering Mediterranean views. You can expect a mix of challenging climbs and more relaxed, low-traffic roads.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40345080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera Promenade – Sitges Beach and Promenade loop from Escultura al collectiu homosexual&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.5-mile (13.6 km) trail leading through coastal promenades and beaches, with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find more challenging touring cycling routes in the region?
A: For a more challenging experience, explore routes that venture into the Garraf Natural Park or along the coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971507538/garraf-buddhist-monastery-and-ribes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Buddhist Monastery and Ribes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.9-mile (40.1 km) route with a 580m elevation gain, offering scenic views and cultural stops. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33729583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Coastal Road – Rat Penat Road loop from Escultura al collectiu homosexual&lt;/a&gt;, featuring challenging coastal roads and significant elevation.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive 16th-century Church of Sant Pere in the old town of Sant Pere de Ribes, or visit the Buddhist Monastery Sakya Tashi Ling within the Garraf Natural Park. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle and the Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3922796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olèrdola Castle and Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, and the elegant Can Puig mansion.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes with scenic viewpoints in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer breathtaking views, especially those traversing the Garraf Natural Park or hugging the coastline. The dramatic limestone peaks of Garraf provide panoramic vistas, while coastal routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33729583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Coastal Road – Rat Penat Road loop&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning sea views. The Foix Reservoir also features spectacular views along its roads.

Q: Are there any good circular touring cycling routes around Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971507538/garraf-buddhist-monastery-and-ribes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Buddhist Monastery and Ribes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Francesc Macià – Bizi Bike Repair Station loop from Vilanova i la Geltrú&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the challenging terrains of the Garraf Natural Park to the picturesque vineyards and the beautiful coastal views. The blend of cultural stops and varied difficulty levels also contributes to a highly enjoyable experience.

Q: Is Sant Pere de Ribes suitable for family-friendly touring cycling?
A: Yes, Sant Pere de Ribes offers options for families. While some routes are challenging, there are easier, flatter sections, particularly along the coast or through the vineyards on low-traffic secondary roads. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40345080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera Promenade – Sitges Beach and Promenade loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for a relaxed family ride.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: While many natural areas in Catalonia are generally dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific park regulations, especially within the Garraf Natural Park, regarding dogs on trails. On less formal paths and quiet country roads, dogs on a leash are often welcome, but ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling speeds and distances.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is well-equipped with places to stop. Sant Pere de Ribes itself has charming cafes and restaurants. Routes passing through the Penedès wine region often go by wineries that offer tastings and refreshments. Additionally, coastal towns like Sitges, accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38220335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitges Beach and Promenade loop&lt;/a&gt;, provide numerous dining options.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes touring cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially for challenging climbs, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended. Winters are generally mild, allowing for cycling, though some higher elevation paths might be cooler.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: Sant Pere de Ribes is well-connected by public transport, making it accessible for cyclists. Buses connect the municipality to nearby towns like Sitges and Vilanova i la Geltrú, which have train stations with services to Barcelona. Check local transport providers for specific routes and bike carriage policies, as these can vary.
tips: Castillo de Ribes, also called castillo de Bell-lloc or castillo de Sota-ribes, located in the municipality of San Pedro de Ribes (Garraf) declared cultural asset of national interest. Dated in the 10th century and is located at the top of a small steep hill.

One of the many stone cabins scattered around the Garraf, this restored stone hut is known as the "Caseta de Vinya". It has a circular plan, covered with a false dome and faces southwest. It is located a few meters from the old road from Vallgrassa to Plana Novella.

The Palau Novella or Garraf Buddhist Monastery is a former Indian house that today is the Sakya Tashi Ling Buddhist monastery. It is located in the Garraf Natural Park, in the Plana Novella urbanization, in the municipality of Olivella.

Of neo-Gothic and modernist style, it was built in the 19th century by Pere Domènech i Grau, an Indian who returned from Cuba after making his fortune there. Years later, the owner, burdened by debt, sold the palace to a community of Augustinian religious, and in 1996 it was acquired by a Buddhist community who restored it and created a museum. The monastery can be visited, more information at https://monjesbudistas.org/
Small cozy town with beautiful old streets and places to stop for refreshments.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging route classified as 'difficult' and a 'hard bike ride'. It requires very good fitness due to significant elevation gain and varied terrain. Some segments may even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, which includes rocky, dry, and broken sections, as well as steep asphalt roads, trails, and gravel, a touring bike capable of handling both paved and unpaved, potentially rough surfaces, would be most suitable for this route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and challenging. You'll encounter rocky, dry, and broken sections, sometimes with overgrown vegetation. There are steep asphalt roads and very steep tarmac climbs, as well as trails and gravel roads within Garraf Park.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque scenery throughout the ride. The route offers beautiful landscapes of vineyards and horizons, a fantastic panorama of the Mediterranean during climbs, and winds through forests, past farmhouses, and alongside crops.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the historic Castillo de Ribes, the unique Garraf Buddhist Monastery, Sakya Tashi Ling (housed in the modernist Palau Novella), The Vinya Hut, and the Parish Church of Sant Pere de Ribes.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Ribes Castle. You would typically look for parking options in Sant Pere de Ribes, near the castle, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Parc del Garraf, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife encounters. Consider the difficulty and length of the ride for your dog's endurance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and the terrain can be dry and exposed. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for Parc del Garraf?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access Parc del Garraf for cycling. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Olivella trail, the Camí Ramader de Marina, and the Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: The research mentions passing farmhouses and feeling removed from others, suggesting limited direct services on the trail itself. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, and plan for refreshments at the start or end points in Sant Pere de Ribes or nearby towns.
